task_completion.description: Dashboard is saved with the correct name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web interface shows the dashboard title as 'Linux Memory Accounting' at the top of the screen.

task_completion.description: First graph is created with the correct title
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'Absolute Memory Breakdown' is visible in the dashboard.

task_completion.description: First graph uses stacked() and aliasByNode() functions
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Absolute Memory Breakdown' graph displays a stacked area chart. The legend below the graph shows shortened metric names (e.g., 'memory-used', 'memory-cached') instead of full paths like 'collectd.*.memory.*'.

task_completion.description: Second graph is created with the correct title
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'True Application Memory Percent' is visible in the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Second graph uses asPercent() to show utilization
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'True Application Memory Percent' graph has a Y-axis scaled for percentages (e.g., 0 to 100) and plots the memory-used metric as a percentage of the total.

task_completion.description: Third graph is created with the correct title
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel titled 'Available Memory' is visible in the dashboard.

integrity.description: Agent used the Graphite web interface to build the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the Graphite composer tree, clicking on metrics, and applying functions via the UI, rather than just writing a script or editing a JSON file.
The graphs show plotted data lines or colored areas, indicating that live metrics from the 'collectd' namespace are successfully being queried and rendered, not just empty graph panels.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake titles
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard titles and graph titles appear naturally within the Graphite UI layout without signs of HTML manipulation via the browser's Inspect Element tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Linux Memory Accounting'. There must be exactly three graphs with the titles: 'Absolute Memory Breakdown', 'True Application Memory Percent', and 'Available Memory'. The metrics must be sourced from the 'collectd' namespace, not 'servers'. Graph 1 must visually reflect a stacked area chart with aliased node names (short names). Graph 2 must visually reflect a percentage scale.
